vulnerability.reference: Content moderation systems rely primarily on text-based filters, not image content analysis.
vulnerability.generated: Content moderation systems rely on text prompts to flag harmful outputs, leaving them incapable of detecting policy-violating images triggered by innocuous prompts.

impact.reference: Increased exposure of users to harmful imagery, undermining platform safety.
impact.generated: Unfiltered circulation of copyrighted or prohibited images through platform channels, causing institutional liability, copyright breaches, and erosion of user safety.

dose_response.reference: Stealthy poisoned training samples create triggered associations, which in content moderation bypass scenarios expose moderators to policy-violating images, leading to increased user harm and moderator burnout.
dose_response.generated: Backdoor data poisoning of text-to-image diffusion models, through platform content moderation systems, triggers the inability of text-prompt-based filters to detect policy-violating images cued by innocuous prompts, leading to unfiltered circulation of copyrighted or prohibited images, causing liability and safety erosion.
